title: Kashmiri Yakhni (Curd-Braised Mutton Stew)

subs.name: bone-in chicken pieces — reduce simmering to 30 minutes
subs.notes: yakhni with chicken is lighter and cooks much faster

subs.name: 100 ml evaporated milk whisked with 2 tbsp lemon juice
subs.notes: the flavour will be less tangy; adjust salt accordingly

subs.name: neutral cooking oil
subs.notes: ghee is integral to the richness of this dish; substitute only if necessary

steps.text: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium flame. Add the asafoetida, then the bay leaves, both types of cardamom, cloves and cinnamon. Let them sizzle for 30 seconds — the ghee will become wonderfully fragrant.
Add the mutton pieces and brown on all sides over high heat, turning every 2 minutes, for about 8 minutes. The surface should be pale brown, not dark — yakhni should remain light in colour.

Add the fennel seeds and dried ginger powder, stir for 30 seconds. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add the whisked curd two tablespoons at a time, stirring constantly and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before the next. This prevents curdling.
Once all the curd is incorporated, pour in 500 ml water and season with salt. Stir gently and bring to a slow boil.

Cover and simmer on the lowest possible flame for 50 minutes, stirring once every 15 minutes. The mutton is done when it falls easily from the bone and the gravy is fragrant and slightly thickened. Do not let the gravy boil vigorously or the yogurt will split.
Taste for salt and serve the yakhni in wide bowls over steamed basmati rice. The gravy should be a pale, almost ivory colour with pools of ghee on top — that is correct and desirable.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mutton → bone-in chicken pieces — reduce simmering to 30 minutes (yakhni with chicken is lighter and cooks much faster); ghee → neutral cooking oil (ghee is integral to the richness of this dish; substitute only if necessary); curd → 100 ml evaporated milk whisked with 2 tbsp lemon juice (the flavour will be less tangy; adjust salt accordingly)
